{"id":"project-LICHEN-2h1i.4","title":"task","description":"## The Problem
LICHEN will collapse at DEFCON density without PHY-level changes.

300 nodes in mutual range:
- 1 announce/sec
- ~40% channel utilization just for routing
- Hidden terminals cause collision cascades

## Solutions (all work on existing hardware)

### TDMA (priority 1)
- Gateway broadcasts time beacon
- Nodes sync to GPS or beacon
- Assigned slots prevent collision
- Pure software, works on all chips

### Adaptive SF
- Near nodes: SF7 (50ms airtime)
- Far nodes: SF10 (500ms airtime)
- 10x less channel time for nearby traffic

### Multi-channel coordination
- 8 channels available in US 915MHz
- Coordinate via gateway assignment
- 8x capacity if done right

### LR-FHSS (SX1262 only)
- Best collision resilience
- Hardware support required
- ~50% of Meshtastic devices support it

### Boot storm mitigation
- Listen 30-60 sec before TX
- Scale initial delay by observed density
- Prevents 300 nodes all announcing at once

## What LICHEN already has
- Announce duplicate suppression
- Trickle DIO with suppression
- Gradient routing (no discovery flood)

These help, but not enough for DEFCON scale.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-17T23:58:44.55518Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-17T23:58:44.55518Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2h1i.4","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2h1i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-17T23:58:44.684037Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
